steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The data shows that full-price restaurant customers leave worse comment ratios than discounted customers. The question asks which principle best explains why higher price (no discount) leads to more negative comments.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The number of people going to gas stations at restaurants is greater than that of people going to gas stations near schools.: states that customers who dine at full price are more likely to be first-time visitors with no loyalty to the restaurant — this introduces information about customer type that is not supported by the passage or data. ✗
  • Choice B — People who go to gas stations at restaurants have higher expectations about the service because of higher price.: Directly explains the pattern: paying full price raises the bar, so the same experience is more likely to disappoint, producing worse comment ratios. ✓
  • Choice C — People are more willing to give good comments to gas stations near schools.: states that customers who receive discounts are more likely to leave comments than those who pay full price — this only re-describes what the data shows about comment frequency without explaining why full-price customers leave worse ratios. ✗
  • Choice D — More gas stations should be established near schools.: states that restaurants should lower prices to improve customer satisfaction — this prescribes a pricing policy that goes beyond the data and does not explain the comment-ratio gap. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
